description: 9 pre-built MCP Stack configurations — each stack recommends exactly 4 servers that work together with documented synergy scores and mismatch warnings. The unsolved problem: which 4 servers do you use together? These stacks answer that.

stacks.name: Full-Stack Web Studio
stacks.description: Build, deploy, and monitor production web applications end-to-end
stacks.useCase: Solo dev or small team shipping a full-stack Next.js app from concept to production

servers.name: filesystem
servers.reason: Read/write project files — the foundation of any code project

servers.name: github
servers.reason: PR management, issue tracking, CI/CD pipeline triggers

servers.name: vercel
servers.reason: Deploy previews on every PR, production deploys on merge

servers.name: postgres
servers.reason: Database migrations, seed data, query debugging

synergy.reason: Filesystem + GitHub covers code lifecycle; Vercel handles deployment; Postgres provides persistent storage. Zero overlap.

stacks.name: AI Research Lab
stacks.description: Research, analyze, and synthesize information with AI-powered search and knowledge graphs
stacks.useCase: Deep research workflows requiring multi-source verification and structured knowledge extraction

servers.name: brave-search
servers.reason: Web search for real-time information discovery

servers.name: fetch
servers.reason: Retrieve and extract content from discovered URLs

servers.name: memory
servers.reason: Persist research findings across sessions as structured knowledge

servers.name: sqlite
servers.reason: Store and query structured research data locally

synergy.reason: Search discovers, Fetch extracts, Memory persists, SQLite structures. Clean pipeline with zero overlap.

stacks.name: Content Engine
stacks.description: End-to-end content creation, optimization, and distribution pipeline
stacks.useCase: Content teams producing SEO-optimized articles, social posts, and newsletters at scale

servers.name: filesystem
servers.reason: Read briefs, write drafts, save final content

servers.name: brave-search
servers.reason: Topic research, competitor analysis, trending queries

servers.name: google-drive
servers.reason: Access brand guidelines, editorial calendars, asset libraries

servers.name: slack
servers.reason: Content approvals, team notifications, review workflows

synergy.reason: Search + Filesystem covers research-to-draft; Google Drive provides source material; Slack enables human-in-the-loop review.

mismatches.warning: Both store files — decide primary storage to avoid split-brain

stacks.name: DevOps Command Center
stacks.description: Deploy, monitor, and respond to incidents across cloud infrastructure
stacks.useCase: Platform teams managing multi-service deployments with observability and incident response

servers.name: github
servers.reason: Code changes, CI/CD triggers, deployment workflows

servers.name: docker
servers.reason: Container management, image builds, compose orchestration

servers.name: kubernetes
servers.reason: Cluster management, scaling, rolling deployments

servers.name: slack
servers.reason: Incident alerts, deployment notifications, runbook access

synergy.reason: GitHub -> Docker -> K8s is the standard build-ship-run pipeline. Slack adds the human notification layer.

stacks.name: Data Pipeline Studio
stacks.description: Ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data at any scale
stacks.useCase: Data engineers building ETL pipelines, running analytics, and creating dashboards

servers.name: postgres
servers.reason: Source and destination for relational data

servers.name: sqlite
servers.reason: Local analysis, prototyping, lightweight queries

servers.name: fetch
servers.reason: Pull data from APIs and web sources

servers.name: filesystem
servers.reason: Read CSVs, write reports, manage data artifacts

synergy.reason: Fetch + Filesystem for ingestion; Postgres for production data; SQLite for analysis sandbox. Clean separation of concerns.

mismatches.warning: Both are SQL databases — use Postgres for production, SQLite for local analysis only

stacks.name: Security Audit Toolkit
stacks.description: Scan, analyze, and remediate security vulnerabilities across code and infrastructure
stacks.useCase: Security teams running vulnerability assessments, dependency audits, and compliance checks

servers.name: filesystem
servers.reason: Read source code, config files, dependency manifests

servers.name: github
servers.reason: Access repos, check branch protections, review security advisories

servers.name: brave-search
servers.reason: Look up CVEs, security advisories, exploit databases

servers.name: docker
servers.reason: Scan container images for vulnerabilities

synergy.reason: Filesystem + GitHub covers code security; Search covers external threat intel; Docker covers container security.

stacks.name: Creative Studio
stacks.description: Design, generate, and manage visual assets with AI-powered creative tools
stacks.useCase: Designers and creators producing images, UI mockups, and visual content at scale

servers.name: filesystem
servers.reason: Save generated images, organize asset libraries

servers.name: fetch
servers.reason: Download reference images, pull design inspiration

servers.name: google-drive
servers.reason: Share creative assets with team, access brand kits

servers.name: slack
servers.reason: Creative review workflows, design critique notifications

synergy.reason: Filesystem for local work; Fetch for sourcing; Google Drive for sharing; Slack for review. Good creative pipeline.

mismatches.warning: Asset storage split — use Drive for shared, filesystem for local

stacks.name: Mobile App Workshop
stacks.description: Design, develop, test, and ship React Native mobile applications
stacks.useCase: Mobile developers building cross-platform apps with React Native and backend APIs

servers.name: filesystem
servers.reason: Read/write app code, manage project structure

servers.name: github
servers.reason: Version control, PR reviews, release management

servers.name: fetch
servers.reason: API testing, backend data integration

servers.name: memory
servers.reason: Persist app architecture decisions and API contracts

synergy.reason: Filesystem + GitHub for code lifecycle; Fetch for API integration; Memory for architectural decisions across sessions.

stacks.name: Unified AI Gateway
stacks.description: 9Router + OWL Proxy Defense + 4-CLI unified gateway — route AI requests through free-tier providers first, with circuit breaker fallback and protocol-first proxy defense
stacks.useCase: Teams and power users managing multiple AI providers (Gemini, Claude, Codex, OpenCode) with unified routing, proxy defense, and credential vault

servers.name: filesystem
servers.reason: Read/write 9Router configs, proxy routing rules, and credential vault files

servers.name: github
servers.reason: Auto-commit proxy config changes, version control for routing rules

servers.name: docker
servers.reason: Run 9Router gateway, sing-box proxy, and Redis cache containers

servers.name: fetch
servers.reason: Health check proxy endpoints, test provider connectivity, monitor token validity

synergy.reason: Filesystem stores configs; GitHub versions them; Docker runs the stack; Fetch monitors health. Complete infrastructure lifecycle with zero overlap.

curatorRules.description: The MCP Stack Curator recommends which 4 servers to use together. Rules: (1) No two servers with identical capabilities, (2) Pipeline bonus when servers form a clear data flow, (3) Complementary bonus when servers cover different layers, (4) Overlap penalty when servers have competing features.
